text:
As part of our ongoing commitment to supporting national talent and strengthening collaboration with the Al Ain Pledges initiative in partnership with NAFIS, we welcomed more than 150 talented candidates to begin the recruitment process at our Operations Centre in Al Ain.

The initiative was met with strong engagement and enthusiasm from attendees, reflecting the growing interest in the career opportunities available and the confidence inspired by this important step forward.
في إطار التزامنا المستمر بدعم الكفاءات الوطنية وتعزيز التعاون مع مبادرة تعهدات العين بالشراكة مع نافس، استقبلنا أكثر من 150 من المرشحين المتميزين لبدء إجراءات التوظيف في مركز العمليات التابع لنا في مدينة العين.

وقد حظيت المبادرة بتفاعلًا قويًا وحماسًا ملحوظًا من الحضور، ما يعكس الاهتمام المتزايد بالفرص المهنية المتاحة والثقة التي عززتها هذه الخطوة المهمة.

text:
Most valuable brands in the Middle East, 2026:

🇸🇦 Saudi Aramco: $47.3B
🇸🇦 ADNOC: $21.1B
🇸🇦 STC (Saudi Telecom): $17.6B
🇦🇪 Etisalat (e&): $16.4B
🇦🇪 Emirates: $10.6B
🇶🇦 Qatar National Bank: $10.4B
🇸🇦 Al-Rajhi Bank: $9.8B
🇸🇦 Saudi National Bank: $6.2B
🇦🇪 Emirates NBD: $6.1B
🇦🇪 First Abu Dhabi Bank: $5.5B

The oil dominance is total.

Aramco at $47.3B is 2.2x the 2nd place brand. 

ADNOC in 2nd place, also an oil company , confirms the pattern.

Both Aramco and ADNOC are operating in an environment where Gulf energy infrastructure has been under direct attack.

Aramco just posted $33.6 billion in quarterly profit. 

STC, Etisalat, and Emirates in the top 5 show that Saudi and UAE diversification is real at the brand level.

But oil still owns the top spots by a margin that no tech company, bank, or airline in the region comes close to closing.

In the Middle East in 2026, brand value is still denominated in barrels.
